habitat: Arctic tundra; meadows; marshes; open grasslands; coastal areas; and agricultural fields
story: Meet the Snowy Owl (Bubo scandiacus), a distinctive bird from the Strigidae family. Preferring habitats such as Arctic tundra; meadows; marshes; open grasslands; coastal areas; and agricultural fields, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Vulnerable (VU), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Snowy Owl serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Snowy Owl enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
